channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Curated geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: 🚢 Trump Announces "Project Freedom" to Escort Ships Through Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The US will launch a military-backed operation to guide stranded commercial vessels through the Strait of Hormuz, with 15,000 personnel and over 100 aircraft committed to the effort as the Iran conflict continues.

item.title: 🇮🇳 Indian Rupee and Bonds Face Pressure from Prolonged US-Iran Conflict
item.description: Stalled efforts to resolve the US-Iran conflict are keeping oil prices elevated, straining capital flows into energy-importing India as the rupee and government bonds face fresh pressure.

item.title: 🛢️ OPEC+ Output Increase Symbolic but Significant Amid Hormuz Crisis
item.description: The seven remaining OPEC+ members agreed to increase output for a third straight month in June, though analysts say the boost is largely symbolic given supply disruptions from the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: ⛪ Rubio to Meet Pope Leo in Rome Following Trump Criticism
item.description: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio will travel to Italy this week to meet Pope Leo, weeks after President Trump criticized the pontiff, in an effort to repair diplomatic relations.

item.title: 🌏 ADB Launches Finance Scheme for Critical Mineral Supply Chains
item.description: The Asian Development Bank unveiled a new financing mechanism to help Asia-Pacific nations develop supply chains for critical minerals used in clean energy, batteries, and electric vehicles.

item.title: 📈 Asian Stocks Hit Record High as Tech Rally Offsets Middle East Tensions
item.description: Asian equities excluding Japan reached an all-time high as technology stocks rallied, with investors shrugging off ongoing geopolitical uncertainty in the Middle East.

item.title: 🇱🇧⚔️ Hezbollah suffers heavy losses in battle to reverse its fortunes in southern Lebanon
item.description: Israel has occupied a chunk of southern Lebanon and displaced thousands of Shi'ite Muslim constituents, with Hezbollah paying a steep price in its efforts to regain ground and influence in the region.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇲🇦⚠️ Two US troops missing during African Lion military exercise in Morocco
item.description: Two U.S. service members participating in the African Lion joint military exercises were reported missing near the city of Tan Tan in southern Morocco, according to U.S. Africa Command and Moroccan authorities.

item.title: 🇵🇱🇺🇸🛡️ Poland says no signals of delays in US Patriot missile deliveries
item.description: Warsaw has not received any indications that possible delays in American weapons deliveries to Poland would affect Patriot air defense systems, the Polish Defence Minister stated amid broader concerns about US military aid flows.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇹🇼🇸🇿 US praises Taiwan as trusted partner ahead of President Lai's Africa visit
item.description: The U.S. State Department called Taiwan a "trusted and capable" partner and praised Taipei's global relationships, including with Eswatini, as President Lai Ching-te visits the southern African kingdom.

item.title: 🇺🇸💨⚠️ Trump administration halts US wind farm projects citing national security
item.description: President Trump's administration has brought U.S. onshore wind development to a halt, citing national security concerns, the Financial Times reported, marking a significant shift in American energy policy.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukrainian drone hits luxury Moscow tower days before Victory Day parade
item.description: A drone attack struck the Mosfilm Tower in central Moscow, marking one of the deepest strikes into the Russian capital residential core. The attack comes just days before the May 9 Victory Day parade.

item.title: 🚢 Three dead from suspected hantavirus outbreak on Atlantic cruise ship
item.description: A suspected hantavirus outbreak aboard the MV Hondius cruise ship has killed three passengers, with one more in intensive care. The ship was traveling from Argentina to the Canary Islands via Antarctica.

item.title: 🇺🇦🇷🇺 Ukraine Strikes Russian Baltic Oil Port of Primorsk in Major Drone Assault
item.description: Ukraine launched a wave of drone attacks on targets across Russia, hitting the Baltic Sea port of Primorsk and setting it on fire, while striking multiple vessels. The attack on Russia's key oil export terminal marks a significant escalation in Ukraine's campaign against Russian energy infrastructure.

item.title: ☢️🇺🇦 IAEA Reports Drone Strike on Zaporizhzhia Nuclear Plant Radiation Lab
item.description: The Russian-controlled Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant informed the International Atomic Energy Agency that a drone targeted its external radiation control laboratory. The incident raises fresh concerns about nuclear safety near the front lines of the Ukraine conflict.

item.title: 🇮🇷⛪ Jailed Iranian Nobel Laureate Narges Mohammadi in Critical Condition
item.description: Nobel Peace Prize laureate Narges Mohammadi remains in critical condition in a cardiac care unit in northwestern Iran after being transferred from prison. Her family's foundation warns that authorities have blocked her transfer to Tehran for specialist care.

item.title: 🇦🇪🛢️ UAE Exits Arab Oil Exporters Group OAPEC
item.description: The United Arab Emirates has left the Organization of Arab Petroleum Exporting Countries (OAPEC), an alliance that does not set production policies. The move comes amid shifting energy dynamics in the Gulf region following the Iran-US conflict.

item.title: 🇯🇵💰 Japanese Yen Steadies as Intervention Fears Linger
item.description: The yen stabilized at the start of Asian trading, strengthening slightly after suspected currency intervention by Japanese authorities last week. Markets remain on alert for further action as the Iran conflict continues to impact global financial markets.

item.title: ⚠️ Iran Warns US Hormuz Operation Would Violate Ceasefire
item.description: A senior Iranian parliament official stated that any US interference in the Strait of Hormuz maritime regime would be considered a ceasefire violation. CENTCOM said the operation would involve destroyers, 100+ aircraft, and 15,000 service members.

item.title: 🛢️ OPEC+ Agrees Third Output Quota Hike Since Hormuz Closure
item.description: OPEC+ has agreed to a third increase in oil production quotas since the Strait of Hormuz was effectively closed due to the Iran war. The increase remains largely theoretical as long as Gulf oil shipments are disrupted.

item.title: 💰 Oil Prices Ease After Trump Hormuz Escort Announcement
item.description: Oil prices fell after President Trump announced the US would help free ships stranded in the Strait of Hormuz. However, prices remained above $100 per barrel as no US-Iran peace deal has been reached.

item.title: ✈️ Israel Approves Purchase of New F-35 and F-15IA Fighter Squadrons
item.description: Israel gave final approval for a plan to purchase two new combat squadrons of F-35 and F-15IA advanced fighter aircraft from Lockheed Martin and Boeing in a deal worth tens of billions of dollars.

item.title: 🇴🇲 Iranian drones strike Oman oil storage facility at Salalah port
item.description: Iran expanded attacks beyond the Strait of Hormuz, hitting fuel storage tanks at Oman's Port of Salalah — a key alternative route for tankers bypassing the blocked strait. The strike marks an escalation in the campaign targeting regional energy infrastructure.

item.title: 🛢️ Oil surges past $120/barrel as Trump rejects Iran overture
item.description: Brent crude hit its highest level of the conflict after Trump maintained the naval blockade of Iranian ports. The UAE announced it would leave OPEC on May 1 to respond to market shortages without cartel constraints.

item.title: 🤖 Pentagon's Office of Net Assessment chief joins Anthropic
item.description: James Baker, who led the Defense Department's long-term strategic analysis office from 2015-2025, joined AI company Anthropic as a "strategist-in-residence." The move comes after the Trump administration temporarily shuttered ONA and designated Anthropic a supply-chain risk.
